title: centaurea calalepis
intro: Centaurea calalepis, also known as the lesser knapweed or scabiosa, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the genus Centaurea and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centaurea calalepis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Centaurea calalepis can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and usage may vary depending on regional regulations and availability.
